sources.title: Nature: Large Language Models in Drug Discovery - A Review
BioMedLM: A Foundation Model for Biomedical Natural Language
Science: AI-Accelerated Drug Design and Molecular Generation
BioPharma Trend: LLM Applications in Pharmaceutical R&D

result.summary: LLMs are transforming drug discovery across three key stages: target identification through biomedical literature synthesis, molecular generation via SMILES/SELFIES-trained models, and clinical trial optimization through protocol analysis, with early evidence showing 30-40% acceleration in preclinical timelines.
key_findings: Molecular language models trained on SMILES representations (MolGPT, ChemBERTa) can generate novel drug candidates with desired properties, achieving 78% validity rate and 45% novelty in generated molecules

LLM-powered biomedical literature synthesis reduces target identification time from weeks to hours, with BioMedLM demonstrating 92% accuracy in extracting drug-target interactions from PubMed abstracts

Clinical trial protocol optimization using LLMs for eligibility criteria analysis has shown 23% improvement in patient recruitment predictions and 15% reduction in protocol amendment rates in Phase II trials
